title: ranunculus trichophyllus subsp. radians
intro: Ranunculus trichophyllus subsp. radians, also known as the water crowfoot or radian water crowfoot, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family Ranunculaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and providing relief from skin irritations. The leaves and stems of the plant can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, although it's essential to exercise caution due to its potential toxicity.
